title: SNMP Community String and OID Enumeration
description: This runbook operationalizes SNMP Community String and OID Enumeration using #onesixtyone , #snmpwalk . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Brute-forces SNMP communities and expands host intelligence via OID walks.
shortDescription: SNMP Community String and OID Enumeration

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes SNMP Community String and OID Enumeration using #onesixtyone , #snmpwalk. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Brute-forces SNMP communities and expands host intelligence via OID walks.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:24; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:26.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on UDP 161.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

onesixtyone -c /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/SNMP/snmp-onesixtyone.txt {IP}
snmpwalk -v1 -c public {IP}
snmpwalk -v2c -c <community string> <FQDN/IP>
extraInfo: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md ; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:24; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:26 .
prerequisites: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on UDP 161 .
